turns.content: "Advancements in AI in the medical field"

[1] [3] 

Advancements in AI in the medical field have led to significant improvements in diagnosis accuracy, efficacy, and standardization. These advancements enable healthcare practitioners to quickly sift through enormous amounts of medical imaging data, finding anomalies, quantifying features, and providing useful information to aid medical professionals in making judgments. Furthermore, AI-based systems can be used to track the evolution of diseases, plan treatments, and highlight particular areas of interest in medical pictures.

[2] 

It is essential to note that AI complements human expertise rather than replacing it. Continuing research and collaboration between AI developers and healthcare practitioners is crucial to ensure a safe and efficient application of AI technologies in clinical settings.

There are several applications of AI in radiology diagnosis, including chest X-ray interpretation for pneumonia, tuberculosis, and COVID-19; CT and MRI analysis for brain tumors, spinal conditions, and musculoskeletal injuries; mammography screening for breast cancer using tools such as iCAD's ProFound AI deployed at over 9,000 sites globally; and CT angiography for vascular disease.

The market is projected to grow significantly due to government-backed initiatives in Asia Pacific.
